channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Politics Friday: Crime, public safety measures drive debates at the Capitol
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guests talk about new public safety bills and criminal justice debates at the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Senate staying up all night to push GOP budget over objections from Democrats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senators kept chugging through an all-night budget “vote-a-rama,” a crucial, if dreaded, step toward unleashing a $340 billion package President Donald Trump's team says it needs for mass deportations and security measures that top the Republican agenda.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, KEVIN FREKING and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Joint Chiefs chairman heads to US-Mexico border to assess rapid military buildup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Gen. CQ Brown, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, is visiting troops along the U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge will consider further blocking Trump administration from freezing funds
item.description: A federal judge will consider a request to further block President Donald Trump’s administration from freezing grants and loans potentially totaling trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Scramble for McConnell's Senate seat underway with signs of a bruising GOP primary ahead
item.description: The scramble to fill Mitch McConnell’s Senate seat in Kentucky began as soon as the Republican revealed he won’t seek reelection in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rep. Burchett: Congress to blame if Trump agenda fails
item.description: GOP Tennessee Rep. Tim Burchett tells NewsNation he knows whose fault it will be if Donald Trump's agenda doesn't succeed.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's DOGE stays on track after pair of federal judge rulings
item.description: A pair of federal judges, in two separate cases, filed rulings that allow DOGE to continue its track of finding government waste and slashing it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York governor won't remove NYC mayor, for now, but plans to increase oversight of City Hall
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Gov. Kathy Hochul won't immediately remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office, but will instead push for increased oversight of City Hall as he faces intense scrutiny over his bribery case and his relationship with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says this congressman would have his 'Total Endorsement' if he runs for Florida governor
item.description: President Donald Trump is making it very clear he would endorse Republican Rep. Byron Donalds if he runs in the 2026 race to succeed term-limited Florida Gov.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell announced Thursday that he won’t seek reelection next year, ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce GOP populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First CDC vaccine meeting under Trump administration is postponed
item.description: The panel of vaccine experts were supposed to hold their first meeting under the Trump administration in late February. It’s not clear when that meeting will now take place.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Judge won't immediately block Trump administration’s abrupt halt to Catholic refugee funding
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday refused to immediately block the Trump administration’s abrupt halt to funding of the nation’s largest private refugee resettlement program in a setback to the U.S. Conference of Catholic Bishops.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration throws out protections from deportation for roughly half a million Haitians
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is throwing out protections that shielded roughly half a million Haitians from deportation, meaning they would lose their work permits and could be eligible to be removed from the country by August.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth circulating list to congressional Republicans of top generals, officers he is interested in firing
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has been circulating a list to congressional Republicans with names of top military generals and officers he wants to fire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: Fellow senators have clapped for Mitch McConnell after he announced he won’t seek reelection next year. The Kentucky Republican is ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration nixes legal representation for thousands of migrant children
item.description: President Trump has made cracking down on immigration a key tenet of his second term, including in some cases the elimination of certain government services.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Gov. Hochul, MTA vow to fight President Trump over congestion pricing
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The MTA is suing the Trump administration after it announced plans to scrap New York City’s congestion pricing toll on Wednesday. A letter sent from U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy to Gov.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Politicians demand cuts to 9/11 health program be reversed
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The 9/11 health program is taking a heavy hit from Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency. Twenty percent of the program's staff were just fired or took buyouts. Already strained workers will now be slower in processing the sick, with far greater wait times and delays.
item.dc:creator: John Muller, PIX 11

item.title: Minnesota bill seeks to disable AI tech used to create nude or sexual images of people
item.description: The plan would require artificial intelligence companies to deactivate a feature that allows users to make people appear naked or engaged in sexual acts in photos or videos without their consent.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Nina Moini, and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: DOGE affiliate may access IRS taxpayer records: Report
item.description: An IRS employee with affiliations to the Department of Government Efficiency may be able to gain access to IRS taxpayer records.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Fentanyl on coroners' radar long before Nevada lawmakers took notice
item.description: The coroner's office knows what's happening in the community long before lawmakers do. That became clear during a discussion of deaths on Nevada's roads in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lutheran Social Service of Minnesota eliminates staff positions
item.description: The organization cites an end to federal funding for refugee resettlement services as the reason for staff cuts. Trump signed the executive order halting the Refugee Admissions Program on his first day in office.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE data access: Sources
item.description: King's exit from the administration is one of several departures of high-ranking officials concerned about DOGE staffers' potential unlawful access to private taxpayer information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Senator Brian Jones to propose bill that would reform sanctuary state practices
item.description: Senator Brian Jones is looking to propose a bill that would shake up current legislation that shapes California as a sanctuary state.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Gregorczyk, Fox 5

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, Fox 5

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WFLA, NBC
